title: FORECAST Act of 2025
plain_summary: The FORECAST Act of 2025 tells NOAA to make weather forecasts better for predicting weather weeks and months ahead of time. The government will spend 28.5 million dollars each year in 2026 and 2027 to help with this work. The bill also creates a new training program to teach people how to use weather prediction tools, computers, and artificial intelligence. This will help train workers who can help make weather forecasts more accurate.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Forecasting Optimization for Robust Earth Climate Analysis and S2S Tracking Act of two thousand twenty-five or the FORECAST Act of two thousand twenty-five.

sections.title: Section 2 Improvements to Weather Research and Forecasting
sections.text: This section makes changes to how the government studies and predicts weather patterns. The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) is the government agency that studies oceans and weather. The Under Secretary is the person who runs NOAA. The law requires NOAA to do several important tasks. First, they must study how to better predict temperature and rainfall patterns for periods of weeks to months ahead. They must collect information from around the world to make weather predictions that people can use and trust. NOAA must improve computer systems that combine different weather prediction models and check how well these predictions work. This includes developing better ways to combine weather data from different sources, creating improved prediction systems, and understanding how oceans, land, snow, and ice affect each other. They must use existing research from other weather scientists to make better predictions. NOAA must work faster to use new modeling technologies developed by researchers, including working with other agencies and groups. They need more funding to help develop systems that predict weather patterns for weeks to months ahead. The agency must figure out how temperature and rainfall predictions relate to severe weather events like tornadoes, droughts, hurricanes, fires, floods, heat waves, ocean heat waves, coastal flooding, winter storms, and other natural disasters. They must also study how these predictions relate to snowpack, sea ice conditions, and permafrost thawing. NOAA must create a website where people can access weather predictions and information for both the whole country and specific regions. The agency should use new technologies like artificial intelligence, machine learning, and unmanned systems like drones. All of these activities must follow the findings from a report that NOAA sent to Congress on July twelfth, two thousand eighteen, called Subseasonal and Seasonal Forecasting Innovation Plans for the Twenty-First Century. When this law talks about Earth system, it means the atmosphere, ocean, land, ice, and related processes and data that affect weather and climate patterns for weeks to months ahead. Congress authorizes spending twenty-eight million five hundred thousand dollars for each of the years two thousand twenty-six and two thousand twenty-seven to carry out these activities. Congress also authorizes whatever money is needed for the modeling team activities within the Earth Prediction Innovation Center or at colleges and universities. This money must be used for high-performance computing, scientific innovation, management and planning, and working with outside groups. The second part of this section creates a new program called the Weather and Earth System Modeling and Data Assimilation Workforce Innovation Program. Data assimilation means combining different types of weather information to create better predictions. NOAA must support education and recruitment of workers and create new training programs. These programs will help train the next generation of researchers, engineers, and practitioners who can work with weather and Earth system technologies. This includes people skilled in new computing systems, artificial intelligence and machine learning, unmanned systems, and next-generation data systems. NOAA can provide money for scholarships, fellowships, and research at colleges and universities in areas that help improve weather prediction systems. NOAA must create and update every year a workforce plan that identifies what workers they need to improve weather technologies and data systems. This plan must identify gaps in funding and expertise, hiring challenges, and policies to reduce employee turnover to avoid staffing changes in the middle of projects. NOAA must send this plan to the Secretary of Commerce and the Director of the Office of Management and Budget. NOAA must use these workforce plans to carry out education and recruitment activities. They can use special hiring programs to recruit and hire people for weather prediction activities. If NOAA finds workforce gaps in their weather prediction processes, they can use available money to carry out their workforce plan and make agreements with colleges, universities, or nonprofit organizations. These agreements help integrate new weather prediction technologies into the larger weather community. NOAA must update their direct hiring authority program to include this new program, fellowship programs, and other relevant programs. Two years after NOAA first provides money to a college, university, or nonprofit organization, they must submit a report to Congress about how effective this money was in improving the quality and preparedness of the weather prediction workforce. This report goes to the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation and the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology. Earth system includes atmosphere, ocean, land, ice, and related processes and data that influence weather and climate patterns for weeks to months ahead. Institution of higher education means colleges and universities as defined in federal education law. Nonprofit entity means organizations that do not make profits as defined in federal defense law. Congress authorizes whatever money is necessary for NOAA to carry out this workforce program.
